footnote: Titles follow the official record from the first professional season, 1932–33 — the league was Division 1 until 2002 — with the 1993 title vacated after the Marseille affair and wartime seasons excluded. Never-champions count from their first top-flight match. The 18 clubs shown are the 2026–27 season's.

teams.name: Angers SCO
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 with Nîmes at Jean-Bouin in their 1956 top-flight debut; two Coupe de France finals since, both lost — the second on a 91st-minute own goal against PSG in 2017.

lastTitle.clinch: drew 1–1 at Guingamp to clinch with a game to spare — Guy Roux's village club completing a league and cup double

teams.name: Stade Brestois 29
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 3–0 at the Vélodrome on opening night 1979 and won once in their first 22 matches; in 2024 they finished third and took tiny Francis-Le Blé to the Champions League.

teams.name: Le Havre AC
teams.firstGameNote: France's oldest club waited six years after the professional league began for a debut — a 3–3 draw at Excelsior Roubaix in 1938; their lone Coupe de France came in 1959, won from the second division.

teams.name: Le Mans FC
teams.firstGameNote: Held Lens 0–0 at Bollaert in their 2003 top-flight debut, just after a young Didier Drogba left town; liquidated in 2013, refounded in the amateur leagues, and back in the top flight for 2026–27.

lastTitle.clinch: drew 1–1 at Auxerre on the final day and took the only title in club history from Metz on goal difference

teams.name: Lille OSC

lastTitle.clinch: won 2–1 at Angers on the final day — Jonathan David and a Yılmaz penalty — to hold off PSG by a single point

teams.lineage: Predecessor Olympique Lillois won the inaugural 1933 title; LOSC's own count starts with the 1944 merger.

teams.name: FC Lorient
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 to Monaco on a 90th-minute Giuly goal in their 1998 debut; the Merlus lifted the 2002 Coupe de France days after being relegated.

lastTitle.clinch: won 3–1 at Auxerre on the final day — Benzema scoring inside a minute — for a record seventh straight title

teams.name: Olympique de Marseille

lastTitle.clinch: beat Rennes 3–1 at the Vélodrome — Heinze, Niang, Lucho González — to end an 18-year wait for title number nine

teams.lineage: Official count is 9: the 1993 title was stripped and left vacant after the match-fixing affair.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Saint-Étienne 2–0 at the Louis II — 18-year-old Kylian Mbappé opening — to end PSG's reign with a game to spare
crowned despite losing 0–3 at home to Angers, as Nîmes' draw at Lille left the Aiglons mathematically uncatchable

teams.name: Paris FC
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Nancy 3–2 in 1972 while inaugurating the brand-new Parc des Princes — then handed it to PSG, the club they had just split from, and waited 46 years to return to the top flight.

lastTitle.clinch: won 2–0 at Lens — Kvaratskhelia, then Mbaye in stoppage time — for a 14th title and a fifth straight, with a match to spare

teams.name: Stade Rennais
teams.firstGameNote: Won 2–1 at Metz on the very first day of French professional football in 1932 — Walter Kaiser scoring — and the founding member is still waiting on a first league title.

lastTitle.clinch: won 3–0 at Lyon on the final day — Roland Wagner scoring twice — three years after playing in Division 2

teams.name: Toulouse FC
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Strasbourg 3–1 in their 1982 top-flight debut at the Stadium; the Violets finally lifted a first major trophy in 2023, thrashing Nantes 5–1 in the Coupe de France final.

teams.name: ES Troyes AC
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 1–0 at PSG on an own goal in their 1999 debut — the third professional club the city of Troyes has produced, now City Football Group-owned and promoted back for 2026–27.
